name: webhook-to-slack
description: Receives structured input, formats it into a Slack Block Kit message using a configurable template, and POSTs to a Slack Incoming Webhook URL. No bot token required -- uses Slack's webhook integration for simple one-way notifications.

SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL.description: Slack Incoming Webhook URL (starts with https://hooks.slack.com/services/...). Obtain from Slack App settings > Incoming Webhooks.

CHANNEL.description: Override the default channel for this webhook (e.g. '#alerts', '#deploys'). Leave empty to use the webhook's default channel.

TEMPLATE.description: Message format template using {{field}} placeholders that are replaced with values from the input JSON. Supports dot notation: {{event.type}}, {{user.name}}. If the resolved template is empty, falls back to a JSON dump of the input.

EMOJI.description: Emoji icon for the bot avatar (e.g. ':warning:', ':white_check_mark:', ':fire:'). Uses Slack emoji syntax.

USERNAME.description: Display name for the bot posting the message.

USE_BLOCKS.description: When true, formats the message as Slack Block Kit (section + context blocks with markdown). When false, sends plain text only.

COLOR.description: Hex color for the attachment sidebar (e.g. '#36a64f' for green, '#ff0000' for red). Only used when USE_BLOCKS is false (attachments mode).

output_shape.success: Boolean: true if Slack accepted the webhook POST (HTTP 200)
output_shape.status_code: HTTP status code returned by Slack
output_shape.channel: The channel the message was posted to (if overridden)
output_shape.message_preview: First 100 characters of the formatted message for traceability

setup_instructions.title: Create a Slack Incoming Webhook
setup_instructions.detail: Go to https://api.slack.com/apps > your app > Incoming Webhooks > Add New Webhook to Workspace. Select the target channel. Copy the webhook URL (starts with https://hooks.slack.com/services/...).

setup_instructions.title: Install the module
setup_instructions.detail: install_module_from_catalog {name: 'webhook-to-slack'}. No secrets required -- the webhook URL is set as node config (Slack webhook URLs are not considered secrets as they are scoped to a single channel).

setup_instructions.title: Configure the message template
setup_instructions.detail: Set TEMPLATE to a format string using {{field}} placeholders matching your input JSON keys. Example: 'Alert: {{alert_name}}\nSeverity: {{severity}}\nDetails: {{details}}'. Dot notation works for nested fields.

setup_instructions.title: Wire into your workflow
setup_instructions.detail: Place this node at the end of a workflow or on an error edge to send notifications. Input should be a JSON object whose fields match the TEMPLATE placeholders.
